skill.name: run-linters
skill.description: Run repository linters after code changes, review reported issues, edit affected files, and repeat checks until lint errors are resolved.
skill.summary: Run configured linters after code changes and fix reported issues before completion.
skill.author: Cloud-Officer

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged possible shell backtick execution and weak cryptography. Review found no cryptographic code and no Ruby backtick execution; the file contains Markdown examples for a fixed linter command. Risk remains medium because the skill intentionally runs repository lint tooling and edits files.

medium_findings.title: Repository Linter Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to execute the fixed linters command and rerun it after fixes. This is expected behavior for the skill, but linter frameworks may execute project-local plugins or scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is explicit and part of the intended workflow. Confidence is not higher because the command name is fixed and no user-controlled shell string is constructed.

medium_findings.title: Automated Source File Modification
medium_findings.description: The skill can read affected files and edit them to fix linter results. This is legitimate for lint remediation, but it can change repository contents and should be reviewed before commit.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and workflow both explicitly permit file reads and edits. The risk is contextual because file modification is also the core purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static finding points to the skill description. No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic code was found at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata describing linting behavior. There are no hash functions, encryption calls, or cryptographic parameters present.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code and a fenced bash example. They do not show Ruby backtick execution or dynamic shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is documentation markup around a fixed command. There is no Ruby code and no evidence of user-controlled command construction.

content.value_statement: Code changes can introduce style, syntax, or quality issues that are easy to miss. This skill runs configured linters and helps fix reported issues before handoff.
actual_capabilities: Runs the configured linters command after a completed set of code changes.
Reviews linter output and identifies files that need fixes.
Reads affected files to understand reported lint errors.
Edits source files to resolve linting issues.
Repeats linter execution until issues are resolved or user guidance is needed.
limitations: Requires a working linters command in the active repository environment.
Does not define or install linter configurations.
May run project-local linter plugins or scripts through the repository toolchain.
Asks before disabling lint rules that appear incorrect.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Run lint checks after a feature branch is complete and fix issues before review.

use_cases.title: Validate Generated Code
use_cases.description: Check AI-generated edits against repository lint rules and correct formatting or style problems.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Before Commit
use_cases.description: Run quality checks after a focused change set and resolve problems before committing.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Lint Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request after a small change set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run linters for the recent code changes and tell me whether anything needs to be fixed.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Lint Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the repository linters, inspect any reported files, and fix lint errors without changing unrelated behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare For Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run linters for this branch, fix valid issues, and summarize what changed before I open a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Linter Failures
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review when failures may be rule or configuration issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run linters, separate real code issues from questionable rule failures, fix safe issues, and ask before disabling any rule.

output_examples.input: Run linters after my API changes.
output_examples.output: Linters completed after one fix pass. Two formatting issues were corrected, and the final run reported no remaining lint errors.

output_examples.input: Check this branch before I commit.
output_examples.output: The linter reported one unused import and one style violation. Both were fixed, then the linter completed successfully.

output_examples.input: Run linting and review any failures carefully.
output_examples.output: The linter found a rule conflict that may require a configuration decision. No rule was disabled, and user input is needed.

best_practices: Run this skill after a complete logical change set, not after every small edit.
Review file edits before committing because lint fixes can affect source behavior.
Use it in trusted repositories with known linter configurations.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to install or redesign the repository lint stack.
Do not run it automatically on untrusted repositories without reviewing tooling.
Do not disable lint rules without explicit user approval.

faq.question: What command does this skill run?
faq.answer: It runs the repository linters command, which is expected to auto-detect active linters.

faq.question: Does it fix lint errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads affected files, edits valid issues, and reruns linters to verify the fixes.

faq.question: Does it install linting tools?
faq.answer: No. The repository must already provide the linters command and its required dependencies.

faq.question: Can it change source files?
faq.answer: Yes. File editing is part of the lint remediation workflow and should be reviewed before commit.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Repository linters can load project-local plugins, scripts, or configuration.

faq.question: Will it disable lint rules?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to ask the user before disabling a rule that appears incorrect.
